title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels from 20+ countries participated in Boston's Sail Boston 250 event.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail, culminating Sail250's final leg with ships arriving from New York.
Thousands of visitors attended to enjoy Boston's maritime history celebration across various city waterfront locations.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox selected shortstop Jake Schaffner with the No. 20 overall pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked as the No. 75 prospect, batted .356 with six home runs and 50 RBIs in 68 games for UNC.
Defensively, Schaffner committed three errors and recorded a .987 fielding percentage, showcasing reliability at shortstop.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston 2026 Grand Parade of Sail features historic ships, led by the USS Constitution, along Boston's waterfront.
The event celebrates maritime history, including participation by Gloucester's schooner Adventure and smaller replica schooners.
Sail Boston marks its 250th anniversary with a multi-generational, international event, highlighting Boston's maritime tradition.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello named AL 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for his 10.35 ERA in eight starts.
Bello's 2026 first-half performance includes 41 earned runs over 35.2 innings and a 2.21 WHIP.
Despite strong relief work, Bello demoted to Triple-A instead of being called back for a start.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox are on a seven-game winning streak, close to American League's final wild-card spot.
The team faces a decision at the 2026 trade deadline: acquire talent or sell assets.
Craig Breslow, navigating the trade deadline, might need success to secure his position.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden in Boston on July 10, 2026, as part of her 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour'.
Her setlist included hits like 'Hips Don't Lie', 'Waka Waka', and 'Whenever, Wherever', alongside tracks from her latest album.
The concert featured elaborate stage props and emphasized themes of resilience and empowerment, marking her comeback after personal challenges.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey announced $18.6M in tax credits for 662 new homes in 6 Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects in cities like Fall River and Worcester will convert historic buildings into apartments using tax credit awards.
The Housing Development Incentive Program targets cities with economic challenges for housing and downtown revitalization.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, of Townsend, Massachusetts, was arrested in Albany, NY, for alleged armed bank robberies in NH and NJ.
Sawyer is accused of stealing $6,000 from St. Mary’s Bank in Nashua, NH, and $12,000 from a Chase Bank in Boonton, NJ.
The arrest was part of Operation Summer Heat 2.0, involving multiple FBI field offices and local police departments.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead near Route 2 in Shelburne, NH after being missing since July 4.
Grondin's body and his Harley-Davidson were discovered by a resident searching for him Thursday night.
The investigation suggests Grondin crashed, and no other person or vehicle was involved, but the incident is still under inquiry.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declared July 11, 2026, as 'Noah Kahan Day' to honor the singer-songwriter's contributions.
Noah Kahan made history as the first artist to headline four consecutive sold-out shows at Fenway Park.
The proclamation highlights Kahan's ties to Massachusetts and his advocacy for mental health awareness.
